title: Conceptualizing urban heat resilience through capacities and a multidimensional perspective: Empirical evidence from formal and informal settlements of Lahore, Pakistan
content: This index-based study examines the heatwave resilience of both formal and informal communities located in Lahore, Pakistan. Resilience is assessed in terms of its capacities and dimensions.
snippet: This index-based study examines the heatwave resilience of both formal and informal communities located in Lahore, Pakistan. Resilience is assessed in terms of its capacities and dimensions.

title: Developing a rapid earthquake impact assessment procedure at European scale
content: This study introduces a comprehensive rapid earthquake impact assessment procedure at the European scale, designed to provide timely damage and loss estimates following seismic events.
snippet: This study introduces a comprehensive rapid earthquake impact assessment procedure at the European scale, designed to provide timely damage and loss estimates following seismic events.

title: Applying a climate information distillation framework to support a climate resilient hydropower sector in Nepal
content: This study applies the Climate Information Distillation Framework (CIDF) to the Nepal hydropower sector.
snippet: This study applies the Climate Information Distillation Framework (CIDF) to the Nepal hydropower sector.

title: Beyond hazards and sectors: governing systemic climate risks
content: The report uses an exploratory comparative analysis of adaptation governance across selected countries and US states to examine both the recognition of systemic risks.
snippet: The report uses an exploratory comparative analysis of adaptation governance across selected countries and US states to examine both the recognition of systemic risks.

title: Post-Disaster Needs Assessment (PDNA) Report: Cyclone Ditwah, Sri Lanka 2025
content: This is a comprehensive assessment that will guide Sri Lanka's recovery and reconstruction efforts following one of the most devastating disasters in its recent history.
snippet: This is a comprehensive assessment that will guide Sri Lanka's recovery and reconstruction efforts following one of the most devastating disasters in its recent history.

title: Diagnosing pandemic disaster risk management through impact chains and forensic analysis: an intertwined DRM model and key lessons from the hospital network in Bucharest, Romania
content: This study aimed to examine the nuanced interplay between pandemic impacts, vulnerabilities, and adaptation options, and how this unfolded across the disaster risk management DRM cycle (preparedness, response, and recovery).
snippet: This study aimed to examine the nuanced interplay between pandemic impacts, vulnerabilities, and adaptation options, and how this unfolded across the disaster risk management DRM cycle (preparedness,

title: Drought’s inequalities: towards inclusive climate adaptation in Zambia
content: The study highlights urgent needs regarding Zambia's drought: integrating informal settlements into city planning; investing in climate-resilient water, sanitation and hygiene (WASH) infrastructure; strengthening community governance and tenure security.
snippet: The study highlights urgent needs regarding Zambia's drought: integrating informal settlements into city planning; investing in climate-resilient water, sanitation and hygiene (WASH) infrastructure;

title: Kamal Kishore: A “Super El Niño” would be a test for our disaster risk governance systems
content: Signs are pointing to a potentially powerful El Niño event — but there is no need for panic. It is an opportunity to stress-test our disaster risk governance systems, and to strengthen protection for the most vulnerable communities.

title: TF1 Info
content: TF1 Group (French: Groupe TF1) is a French media holding company. Its best-known property is the broadcast network TF1
snippet: TF1 Group (French: Groupe TF1) is a French media holding company. Its best-known property is the broadcast network TF1

title: Popular myths about climate adaptation journalists should know
content: Adaptation is not a substitute for mitigation. Cutting emissions remains indispensable to preventing the worst outcomes of climate change. Even if we succeed in limiting warming to 1.5°C, we will still face more floods, heatwaves, and ecosystem losses.
snippet: Adaptation is not a substitute for mitigation. Cutting emissions remains indispensable to preventing the worst outcomes of climate change. Even if we succeed in limiting warming to 1.5°C, we will

title: Why science communication is essential for climate justice
content: Climate science and adaptation strategies can only make an impact in the real world, when people can understand and act upon them.
snippet: Climate science and adaptation strategies can only make an impact in the real world, when people can understand and act upon them.

title: Ethiopian communities are using low‑cost methods to restore eroded land
content: Nearly a quarter of the land in Ethiopia is degraded. It has lost its quality and can no longer grow crops, support plant life, or hold water as it used to.
snippet: Nearly a quarter of the land in Ethiopia is degraded. It has lost its quality and can no longer grow crops, support plant life, or hold water as it used to.

title: Climate change may shift hailstorms towards Earth’s poles – study
content: Two new studies shed light on how hail might change as the world warms.
snippet: Two new studies shed light on how hail might change as the world warms.

title: Canada’s aerial wildfire‑fighting plan is a start — but it is not yet a strategy
content: The Canadian government recently announced that it will lease a fleet of 10 firefighting aircraft and other support assets to be deployed for the 2026 wildfire season.
snippet: The Canadian government recently announced that it will lease a fleet of 10 firefighting aircraft and other support assets to be deployed for the 2026 wildfire season.

title: Intensification of atmospheric rivers bringing heavy rainfall to Japan
content: Researchers at University of Tsukuba have discovered that influenced by global warming and the strengthening of the North Pacific Subtropical High, the intensity of water vapor transport in ARs has increased by approximately 8% over the past 42 years.
snippet: Researchers at University of Tsukuba have discovered that influenced by global warming and the strengthening of the North Pacific Subtropical High, the intensity of water vapor transport in ARs has

title: Teaching event legacy of the NSW 1952 bushfires
content: The 1952 bushfires in New South Wales (NSW) stand as one of the most significant events in Australia’s bushfire history, not for their death toll, but for the enduring lessons they offered in fire management and mitigation.
snippet: The 1952 bushfires in New South Wales (NSW) stand as one of the most significant events in Australia’s bushfire history, not for their death toll, but for the enduring lessons they offered in fire

title: El Niño 2026: a closing window for anticipatory action in Latin America and the Caribbean
content: The Technical Working Group on Anticipatory Action in Latin America and the Caribbean has published a new briefing on El Niño and anticipatory action in the region.
snippet: The Technical Working Group on Anticipatory Action in Latin America and the Caribbean has published a new briefing on El Niño and anticipatory action in the region.

title: Explained: how El Niño impacts health
content: According to the World Meteorological Organization (WMO), an El Niño event is expected to develop from mid-2026, reshaping global weather, influencing temperatures, rainfall, drought, and extreme weather patterns across the world.

title: From coverage to payouts: tracking pre-arranged finance country by country
content: A new interactive dashboard brings disaggregated, country-level data together for the first time so that governments, practitioners and researchers can see not just how much financial protection exists, but how it has actually performed.

title: Public warning systems: Intersec launches accessibility frameworks to remove barriers to global adoption
content: Intersec, the global leader in mobile metadata solutions and public warning systems (PWS), announced the launch of its "Accessibility Frameworks."
snippet: Intersec, the global leader in mobile metadata solutions and public warning systems (PWS), announced the launch of its "Accessibility Frameworks."

title: Regional Sales Managers – Early Warning Systems (Middle East, Asia-Pacific & Latin America)
content: Intersec is hiring five Regional Sales Managers across the Middle East, Asia-Pacific, and Latin America to drive the deployment of early warning and public alerting solutions in the world's most disaster-exposed regions.

title: Innovatek
content: Innovatek started in 1998 as a technology transfer event management company and has well over two decades within the industry.
snippet: started in 1998 as a technology transfer event management company and has well over two decades within the industry.

title: Systemic climate adaptation framework
content: This report explores how, and to what degree, adaptation policies and governance arrangements are beginning to acknowledge and respond to the systemic nature of climate change.
snippet: This report explores how, and to what degree, adaptation policies and governance arrangements are beginning to acknowledge and respond to the systemic nature of climate change.

title: Brazil readiness assessment: Delivering anticipatory social protection
content: This study uses the ASPIRE tool to evaluate the readiness and effectiveness of social protection systems in Brazil, identifying gaps and opportunities to improve anticipatory measures and climate resilience.
snippet: This study uses the ASPIRE tool to evaluate the readiness and effectiveness of social protection systems in Brazil, identifying gaps and opportunities to improve anticipatory measures and climate

title: Life as a locust officer: safeguarding crops and communities from desert locust
content: In this interview with PreventionWeb, Ali Babalifashki, Desert Locust Information Officer at the Plant Protection Organization of the Islamic Republic of Iran, shares insights from the field on managing locust risks and protecting vulnerable communities.

title: Evaluating the skill of AI and physics-based models for February 2020 North Atlantic storm hazards
content: In this study, the authors compare the performance of data-driven models to physics-based models in forecasting the February 2020 storm series over the United Kingdom.
snippet: In this study, the authors compare the performance of data-driven models to physics-based models in forecasting the February 2020 storm series over the United Kingdom.

title: Embedding risk finance in integrated national financing frameworks
content: This policy note explores how Integrated National Financing Frameworks (INFFs) can support more resilient financing by embedding climate risk diagnostics and climate disaster risk finance and insurance (CDRFI) mechanisms into national financing strategies
snippet: This policy note explores how Integrated National Financing Frameworks (INFFs) can support more resilient financing by embedding climate risk diagnostics and climate disaster risk finance and

title: Early warning! Early action! Early finance?
content: This special issue is about drawing lessons about recognising the importance of acting early, learning from practice, and strengthening the systems that make early action possible.
snippet: This special issue is about drawing lessons about recognising the importance of acting early, learning from practice, and strengthening the systems that make early action possible.

title: Using El Niño Impact Analysis to Inform Policy, Early Action and Risk-Informed Planning in Southern Africa
content: FAO’s El Niño work showed how combining multiple data sources, including agricultural losses and damages, sharpened early warning and made early action more targeted and effective across Southern Africa.
snippet: FAO’s El Niño work showed how combining multiple data sources, including agricultural losses and damages, sharpened early warning and made early action more targeted and effective across Southern

title: Worldwide job losses due to natural hazards
content: This study quantifies job losses due to floods, earthquakes, wind, storm surges, tsunamis, and heat across 132 countries, using a full-time job equivalent loss estimation approach.
snippet: This study quantifies job losses due to floods, earthquakes, wind, storm surges, tsunamis, and heat across 132 countries, using a full-time job equivalent loss estimation approach.

title: Natural hazards cost the world 90 million jobs every year
content: When we think about disasters, we often picture damaged buildings, flooded streets, or destroyed infrastructure. What is less visible, but just as devastating, is what happens to people’s jobs and livelihoods.
snippet: When we think about disasters, we often picture damaged buildings, flooded streets, or destroyed infrastructure. What is less visible, but just as devastating, is what happens to people’s jobs and

title: Scaling the dangers of dust storms
content: ASU-led team develops first large-scale ranking system to raise awareness about the dangers of Phoenix dust storms.
snippet: ASU-led team develops first large-scale ranking system to raise awareness about the dangers of Phoenix dust storms.

title: Study shows wetlands loss has increased residential flood insurance claim payments by $10 billion
content: By calculating the value of wetlands in reducing flood damage, findings can inform federal and state land use decisions, wetland protection, benefit-cost analysis and flood insurance policy.
snippet: By calculating the value of wetlands in reducing flood damage, findings can inform federal and state land use decisions, wetland protection, benefit-cost analysis and flood insurance policy.

title: Prepare for El Niño
content: A new WMO El Niño/La Niña Update indicates an 80% likelihood of an El Niño event during June–August 2026. Probabilities for this to continue until at least November are near or above 90%.
snippet: A new WMO El Niño/La Niña Update indicates an 80% likelihood of an El Niño event during June–August 2026. Probabilities for this to continue until at least November are near or above 90%.

title: Trees and greenery can cool cities by as much as 18°C – but only if they’re the right type
content: Cities around the world are planting more trees to cope with rising urban heat. But our research shows trees alone are often not enough.
snippet: Cities around the world are planting more trees to cope with rising urban heat. But our research shows trees alone are often not enough.

title: A simple — yet expensive — way to climate-proof the grid: Bury the power lines
content: Underground power lines are far less vulnerable to extreme weather, but burying them doesn't come cheap. After a historic ice storm in northern Michigan, utilities there are reconsidering the cost.
snippet: Underground power lines are far less vulnerable to extreme weather, but burying them doesn't come cheap. After a historic ice storm in northern Michigan, utilities there are reconsidering the cost.

title: How a monster ocean heatwave could fuel a super El Niño
content: The chance of an El Niño event emerging by July is now over 80 percent, which will likely make 2026 one of the hottest years on record.
snippet: The chance of an El Niño event emerging by July is now over 80 percent, which will likely make 2026 one of the hottest years on record.

title: Debugging: Google requests permission to release 32m mosquitoes in California and Florida
content: Company asks US government to release army of sterile male mosquitoes to lower number of illness-spreading bugs.
snippet: Company asks US government to release army of sterile male mosquitoes to lower number of illness-spreading bugs.

title: Krakatau and the architecture of vulnerability: Colonial governance, risk perception, and disaster impact
content: This article uses the 1883 eruption of Krakatau as a historical case study to examine how social vulnerability, colonial governance, and failures in risk communication affected the scale of disaster impacts.
snippet: This article uses the 1883 eruption of Krakatau as a historical case study to examine how social vulnerability, colonial governance, and failures in risk communication affected the scale of disaster
